quiz.title: Unit 2 Quiz 7, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Electron Configuration and Atomic Shells
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Particle Charges, Electron Shells (K, L, M, N), Maximum Electron Capacity, Electron Configuration, Valence Electrons, Ions vs Neutral Atoms

questions.question: What is the charge of a proton?
questions.explanation: A proton has a positive charge of +1. It is located in the nucleus of an atom.

questions.question: What is the charge of a neutron?
questions.explanation: A neutron has no electrical charge (neutral). It is located in the nucleus along with protons.

questions.question: What is the charge of an electron?
questions.explanation: An electron has a negative charge of -1. It orbits the nucleus in electron shells.

questions.question:
Two particles P and Q have the composition shown in the table.

| Particle | Number of Electrons | Number of Neutrons | Number of Protons |
|----------|---------------------|--------------------|-------------------|
| P        | 12                  | 12                 | 10                |
| Q        | 18                  | 18                 | 16                |

The particles P and Q are:
questions.explanation:
P: protons = 10 → atomic number 10 (Neon); electrons = 12 → more electrons than protons → negative ion.
Q: protons = 16 → atomic number 16 (Sulfur); electrons = 18 → more electrons than protons → negative ion.
Both are negative ions.

questions.question: The maximum number of electrons that can occupy the K shell is:
questions.explanation: The K shell (first energy level, n=1) can hold a maximum of 2 electrons according to the formula 2n² where n=1: 2(1)² = 2.

questions.question: The L shell can hold a maximum of:
questions.explanation: The L shell (second energy level, n=2) can hold a maximum of 8 electrons according to the formula 2n² where n=2: 2(2)² = 8.

questions.question: The M shell can accommodate up to:
questions.explanation: The M shell (third energy level, n=3) can hold a maximum of 18 electrons according to the formula 2n² where n=3: 2(3)² = 18.

questions.question: The electron configuration of potassium (Z = 19) is:
questions.explanation: Potassium has atomic number 19, meaning 19 electrons. The electron configuration follows the 2, 8, 8, 1 pattern because after filling K (2) and L (8) shells, the M shell can hold up to 18 electrons, but the next electron goes into the N shell (4s orbital) before the M shell is completely filled due to energy level ordering.

questions.question: Which of these shells has the highest energy for a given atom?
questions.explanation: Electron shells are labeled K, L, M, N in order of increasing distance from the nucleus. As distance increases, energy increases. The N shell is the farthest from the nucleus and therefore has the highest energy among these options.

questions.question: The N shell can hold a maximum of:
questions.explanation: The N shell (fourth energy level, n=4) can hold a maximum of 32 electrons according to the formula 2n² where n=4: 2(4)² = 32.

questions.question: An atom has an electron configuration 2, 8, 1. Which shell contains the valence electron?
questions.explanation: The electron configuration 2, 8, 1 shows electrons in K shell (2), L shell (8), and M shell (1). The valence electron is in the outermost shell, which is the M shell.

questions.question: Which element has the electron configuration 2, 8, 7?
questions.explanation: Electron configuration 2, 8, 7 totals 17 electrons, which corresponds to atomic number 17 — Chlorine (Cl).

questions.question: The K shell is closest to the:
questions.explanation: The K shell (first energy level) is closest to the nucleus. It has the lowest energy and is filled first.

questions.question: Which shell is the third energy level?
questions.explanation: K = 1st energy level, L = 2nd energy level, M = 3rd energy level, N = 4th energy level.

questions.question: How many electrons are in the L shell of sulfur (Z = 16)?
questions.explanation: Sulfur (atomic number 16) has electron configuration 2, 8, 6. The K shell has 2 electrons, L shell has 8 electrons, and M shell has 6 electrons.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered electron shells and configurations! ⚛️📊
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the maximum electron capacities for each shell.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on identifying valence electrons and calculating electron configurations.
scoreMessages.0: Please review electron shells, their maximum capacities, and how to write electron configurations.
